title: Negative entropy in scrambling black holes
abstract: We present a microscopic statistical-mechanical foundation for interpreting the horizon area of a scrambling black hole as coherent information, equivalently negative conditional quantum entropy, in Hawking's pair-creation picture. We derive the entropy increase induced in a black hole when an infalling object is absorbed and scrambled into its microscopic degrees of freedom. Up to finite-reservoir corrections, this increase takes a canonical form at the Hawking temperature, regardless of the entropy carried by the infalling object. Applying this entropy formula to an incoming mode paired by time reversal with an outgoing Hawking radiation mode, we show that their partition-function contributions cancel in the coherent-information balance associated with the horizon area. The resulting area response is then determined only by the energy flux, in agreement with the black-hole first law.
summary: This paper provides a statistical-mechanical basis for understanding black hole entropy as coherent information. It explains how the absorption of even pure-state particles increases the horizon area and shows that the thermodynamic first law is maintained through the cancellation of quantum partition functions during Hawking radiation.
main_problem: Resolving inconsistencies in the coherent-information interpretation of black hole entropy, specifically explaining how the area increases when pure-state particles are absorbed and how the first law of black hole mechanics is preserved.
main_result: The author derives a microscopic statistical-mechanical foundation showing that the entropy increase from scrambling depends on energy flux, and proves that partition-function contributions from Hawking pairs cancel out to recover the standard black-hole first law.
method: The study employs microcanonical state-counting, Taylor expansions of entropy around initial energy, and the application of grand-canonical ensembles and partition functions within Hawking's pair-creation framework.
model_or_system: A scrambling Schwarzschild black hole modeled as a system composed of a positive subsystem (B+) and a negative subsystem (B-) containing negative-frequency bosons, utilizing Hawking's pair-creation picture.
key_observables: Horizon area (A_B), Hawking temperature (T_H), entropy increase (delta s_B+), and energy flux.
important_parameters: Hawking temperature, energy (E_P), angular momentum (L_P), charge (Q_P), and the scrambling process efficiency.
main_assumptions: Assumes a 'small' infalling particle to allow for Taylor expansion, utilizes a leading reservoir approximation, and assumes the black hole scrambles infalling matter into its microscopic degrees of freedom.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the problem of entropy interpretation, introduces the partitioned black hole model, derives the entropy increase formula via microcanonical counting, demonstrates the cancellation of partition functions in Hawking radiation, and concludes by recovering the black-hole first law.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
